DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api artifact-reference best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Artifact Reference Guideline

Documents a relationship between the current artifact and another artifact in the system. Used by patterns to reference participating components (with their roles), by styles to reference implementing token groups, and by any artifact that needs to express a named, typed relationship to another DSDS artifact. Each artifact reference guideline describes a single relationship — use multiple guidelines to reference multiple artifacts.

Source: guidelines/artifact-reference.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • artifactReferenceEntry
  • artifactReference

artifactReferenceEntry

A single reference to another DSDS artifact, with an optional description of the role it plays in the current context.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredThe name of the referenced artifact (e.g., 'button', 'form-field', 'color-text', 'spacing'). MUST match the `name` property of a documented DSDS artifact.
rolestringoptionalA short description of the role this artifact plays in the current context (e.g., 'Primary navigation trigger', 'Error summary container', 'Provides semantic text color tokens'). When omitted, the relationship is a general association without a specific role.
requiredbooleanoptionalWhether this artifact is required for the parent to function correctly. When false or omitted, the artifact is an optional enhancement or supplementary reference.

artifactReference

Documents relationships between the current artifact and other artifacts in the system. Contains a categorized list of referenced artifacts — each with a name, an optional role description, and an optional required flag. The `referenceType` property classifies the nature of the relationship (e.g., 'component' for pattern-to-component references, 'token-group' for style-to-token-group references). Custom reference types are permitted for system-specific relationship categories.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"artifact-reference"requiredIdentifies this guideline as an artifact reference specification.
referenceTypestringrequiredThe category of artifacts being referenced. Classifies the nature of the relationship. Standard values: 'component' (references to UI components that participate in a pattern or are related to the current artifact), 'token-group' (references to token groups that implement a style), 'pattern' (references to interaction patterns), 'style' (references to visual style guidelines), 'token' (references to individual tokens). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.
referencesartifactReferenceEntry[]requiredThe artifacts being referenced. Each entry identifies a single artifact by name and optionally describes its role and whether it is required.
Min items: 1
descriptionrichTextoptionalAn optional overview of the relationship — why these artifacts are referenced, how they relate to the current artifact, and any constraints on their usage together.

References: richText, artifactReferenceEntry
